page: Amazon Web Services c7gn.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 48 physical cores on an arm64 architecture using the AWS Neoverse-V1 model running at 2.6 GHz. The instance is allocated 48 dedicated vCPUs with a single thread per core, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Local storage is not included, meaning storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes.

A key differentiator of the c7gn.12xlarge is its baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps, making it highly optimized for network-intensive workloads. In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core performance, particularly in stress-ng multi-core tests and Geekbench multi-core metrics. Its memory subsystem is highly efficient, achieving a top-tier PassMark memory score and strong read/write memory bandwidth across both cached and uncached large blocks. It also shows strong performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput.

While multi-core and memory bandwidth performance are highly competitive, single-core performance metrics, such as single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the average tier. Additionally, the instance lacks x86-specific instruction sets like AVX or AMX, relying instead on its ARMv8 architecture with native AES cryptographic support. Given its high network throughput and balanced compute-to-memory ratio, the c7gn.12xlarge is well-suited for network appliances, distributed databases, containerized microservices, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.12xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 96.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core) and lacks local storage. The instance is highly optimized for networking, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory performance and strong multi-core capabilities, including high-throughput database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving. However, single-core performance and cryptographic execution remain in the average tier, and the ARM architecture lacks x86 instruction sets like AVX. This instance is best suited for network-heavy applications, distributed databases, and multi-threaded compute workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.12xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring 48 Graviton cores, 96 GB RAM, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.12xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 48 Graviton cores, 96 GB RAM, and 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring high-speed network throughput, dedicated physical cores, and strong multi-core memory bandwidth for demanding distributed workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 cores on arm64 architecture.
Equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM and a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps.
Delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and strong multi-core benchmark performance.
Lacks local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions.
Optimized for network appliances, distributed databases, and high-throughput web serving.
